SEARCH

Uses of Class
com.nib.commons.io.AbstractFile

Packages that use AbstractFile
Package
Description
com.nib.commons
Common utilities and core runtime components for the NIB application framework.
com.nib.commons.io
Provides core Input/Output (I/O) components and abstractions for handling various file organizations and access methods.
com.nib.commons.io.catalog
Provides catalog management mechanisms for I/O operations.
com.nib.commons.io.exceptions
Provides exception classes for I/O operations.
com.nib.commons.io.vsam
Provides classes and interfaces for emulating VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file organizations.
com.nib.scripting.utils.dbutils
DB API
com.nib.scripting.utils.nibsort
SORT

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    DummyFile
    Represents a dummy file.
    class 
    SequentialFile
    The SequentialFile class represents a file that handles sequential access operations.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.encoding(EncodingType encoding)
    Sets the encoding type for the file and returns the current instance.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.exceptionsDisabled()
    Disables exception handling for the current file instance.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.exceptionsEnabled()
    Enables exception handling for the current file instance.
    AbstractFile

    Assigns the file status field and returns teh current instance.
    AbstractFile

    Assigns the file status field and returns teh current instance.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.fileStatusIs(NZoned fileStatus)
    Assigns the file status field and returns teh current instance.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.recordContains(int min, int max)
    Specifies that record contains a number of bytes in the specified range.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.recordIs(AbstractNField record)
    Assigns a record to the file using the specified field.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.recordIs(AbstractNField record, EncodingType encoding)
    Assigns a record to the file using the specified field and encoding.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.recordIs(AbstractNField record, NInteger recordLength)
    Assigns a record to the file using the specified field and record length.
    AbstractFile
    AbstractFile.recordIs(AbstractNField record, NInteger recordLength, EncodingType encoding)
    Assigns a record to the file using the specified field, length and encoding.

  • Uses of AbstractFile in com.nib.commons.io.vsam

    Subclasses of AbstractFile in com.nib.commons.io.vsam
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    AbstractEntrySequentialFile
    Abstract class representing an entry-sequential VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file.
    class 
    AbstractKeySequentialFile
    Represents an abstract base class for Key-Sequenced Data Set (KSDS) and Alternate Index (AIX) file handling within a VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) system.
    class 
    AbstractRecordRelativeFile
    Represents an abstract base class for handling relative record datasets (RRDS) in a VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file organization.
    class 
    AbstractVsamFile
    AbstractVsamFile serves as a base class for implementing various types of VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file operations.
    class 
    EntrySequentialFile
    Represents a sequential file for managing file I/O operations within a specific context or database connection.
    class 
    KeySequentialFile
    Represents a key-sequential file object, facilitating operations such as reading, writing, and modifying records within the file.
    class 
    RecordRelativeFile
    The RecordRelativeFile class extends the functionality of AbstractRecordRelativeFile and provides methods to handle relative record file operations, including reading, writing, rewriting, and deleting records.
